definition: QE compresses term premium 1.5-2.5x more than QT adds back because QE operates during stress when risk aversion amplifies the portfolio balance channel. This asymmetry means QT-era term premium remains lower than pre-QE levels even after significant balance sheet reduction.
evidence: 1.5-2.5x asymmetry ratio between QE compression and QT decompression
QE operates during stress when risk aversion amplifies portfolio balance channel
Basis trade absorption of ~half of QT duration redistribution compounds the asymmetry
open_questions: Is the asymmetry ratio stable across different QT speeds?
Does the asymmetry imply a permanent regime shift in term premium?
How does the ratio interact with fiscal supply dynamics?
